digest.component_or_area: store compatibility record and fail-closed capability gate
propositions: Add a committed store-level compatibility record (format_version + required_capabilities) at a fixed path, written atomically, that a v1.0 binary reads before any mutating or externally-publishing operation, failing closed on unknown version or unrecognized required capability while keeping reads and diagnostics available.

The gate is placed at two structural choke points — inside write_lock acquisition (covering all store mutations) and immediately before the outbound Jira push call — rather than an enumerated list of call-sites, with a lint/coverage test grepping every write_lock( site to catch un-gated write paths.

Four record states are defined explicitly: absent means implicit legacy version 0 and passes through unblocked, present-and-compatible passes, present-and-incompatible fails closed, and corrupt/unreadable fails closed (never silently treated as absent).

An idempotent ensure unit opportunistically writes the record when absent at init/fsck-repair/MCP-boot, but because run_ensures is fail-silent the covered-write path must not depend on the sweep running — a missed sweep defers stamping without blocking writes.

The reader must ship in v1.0 because an already-installed v1.0 binary cannot be retrofitted to inspect a record whose semantics were not defined in v1.0, so it must already refuse unknown required capabilities.

Rollback safety requires the committed record to remain byte-unchanged and be treated as an ignored-unknown file by a pre-v1.0 binary, with a documented compensating path to delete .store-compat.json if a pre-v1.0 binary would parse-and-error on it.
